verses:
śaraṇāgatera prārthanā
“tava dāsyasukhaikasaṅgināṁ
bhavaneṣvastvapi kīṭajanma me
itarāvasatheṣu māsmabhūdapi
janma caturmukhātmanā”
(yāmunācārya)

“kāmādīnāṁ kati na katidhā pālitā durnideśā⋅
steṣāṁ jātā mayi na karuṇā, na trapā nopaśāntiḥ
utsṛjyaitānatha jadupate sāmprataṁ labdha⋅buddhi⋅
stvatpādābjaṁ śaraṇamagamaṁ sarva⋅lokaika⋅nāthaṁ

naitanmanastava kathāsu vikuṇṭha⋅nātha
samprīyate duritaduṣṭamasādhu tīvram
kāmāturaṁ harṣaśokabhayaiṣaṇārtaḥ
tasmin kathaṁ tava gatiṁ vimṛṣāmi dīnaḥ’
(śrī.bhā. 7.9.39)

durita dūṣita mama asādhu mānasa
kāma⋅harṣa⋅śoka⋅bhaya⋅eṣaṇāra vaśa

tava kathā⋅rati kise hoive āmāra?
kise kṛṣṇa tava līlā kariva vicāra?

“jihvakato‛cyuta vikarṣati māvitṛptā
śiśno‛nyatastvagudaraṁ śravaṇaṁ kutaścit
ghrāṇo‛nyataścapaladṛk kva ca karmaśakti⋅
rvahvāḥ sapatnya iva gehapatiṁ lunanti”
(śrī.bhā. 7.9.40)

jihvā ṭāne rasa prati, upastha kadarthe
udara bhojane ṭāne, viṣama anarthe

carma ṭāne śayyādite, śravaṇa⋅kathāya
ghrāṇa ṭāne surabhite, cakṣu dṛśye jāya

karmendriya karme ṭāne, vahu patnī jathā
gṛhapati ākarṣaya, mora mana tathā

emata avasthā mora śrī⋅nanda⋅nandana
kirūpe tomāra līlā kariva smaraṇa?

“tadastu me nātha sa bhūribhāgo
bhave‛tra vānyatra tu vā tiraścām
jenāhamekoihapi bhavajjanānāṁ
bhūtvā niṣeve tava pāda⋅pallavam”
(śrī.bhā. 10.14.30)

ei brahma⋅janmei vā anya kona bhave
paśupakṣī ha’ye janmi tomāra vibhave

eimātra āśā, tava bhaktagaṇa⋅saṅge
thāki’ tava padasevā kari nānā raṅge

“ko nvīśa te pādasarojabhājāṁ
sudurlabho‛rtheṣu caturaṣvapīha
tathāpi nāhaṁ pravṛṇomi bhūman
bhavatpadāmbhojaniyevaṇotsukaḥ”
(śrī.bhā. 3.4.15)

kṛṣṇa! tava pāda⋅padme bhakti āche jā̐ra
caturvarga⋅madhye kivā aprāpya tā̐hāra

tathāpi tomāra padasevā mātra cāi
anya kona arthe mora prayojana nāi

“na kāmaye nātha tadapyahaṁ kvacinna
jatra juṣmaccaraṇāmvujāsavaḥ
mahattamāntarhṛdayānmukhacyuto
vidhatsva karṇāyutameṣa me varaḥ”
(śrī.bhā. 4.20.24)

jāhāte tomāra padasevā⋅sukha nāi
sei vara āmi nātha kabhu nāhi cāi

bhaktera hṛdaya haite tava guṇa⋅gāna
śunite ayuta karṇa karaha vidhāna

“na nākapṛṣṭhaṁ na ca pārameṣṭhyaṁ
na sārvabhaumaṁ na rasādhipatyam
na jogasiddhīrapunarbhavaṁ vā
samañjasa tvā virahayya kāṅkṣe”
(śrī.bhā. 6.11.25)

svarga, parameṣṭhī⋅sthāna, sārvabhauma pada
rasātala⋅ādhipatya, jogera sampad

nirvāṇa ityādi jata chāḍi’ sevā tava
nāhi māgi, e mora pratijñā akaitava

“ahaṁ hare tava pādaika⋅mūla⋅
dāsānudāso bhāvitāsmi bhūyaḥ
manaḥ smaretāsupatergaṇāṁste
gṛṇīta vāk karma karotu kāyaḥ”
(śrī.bhā. 6.11.24)

chinu tava nityadāsa, gale vā̐dhi’ māyā⋅pāśa,
saṁsāre pāinu nānā⋅kleśa

ebe punaḥ kari āśa, hañā tava dāsera dāsa,
bhaji’ pāi tava bhaktileśa

prāṇeśvara! tava guṇa, smaruka mana punaḥ punaḥ,
tava nāma jihvā karuka gāna
karadvaya tava karma, kariyā labhuka śarma,
tava pade sa̐pinu parāṇa
en_translation:
Prayer of the Surrendered Soul "May I be born even as a worm in the house of those who are attached only to the happiness of serving You, but let me not be born even as a Lord Brahmā in a house where You are not worshipped."

"I have obeyed the wicked commands of lust and other enemies in so many ways and for so long. They have no mercy, no shame, and give me no peace. O Lord of the Yadus! Abandoning them, and now having regained my intelligence, I have come to You for fearless shelter. Please engage me in Your personal service."

"O Lord of Vaikuṇṭha! My mind does not take pleasure in topics about You because it is polluted by sin, wicked, and very intense. It is afflicted by lust and tormented by happiness, distress, fear, and hankering. How can I, who am so wretched, think of Your path?"

My wicked mind is polluted by sins and controlled by lust, happiness, grief, fear, and hankering.

How will I develop attachment to topics about You? How, O Kṛṣṇa, will I contemplate Your pastimes?

"O Infallible One! My unsatisfied tongue drags me in one direction, my genitals in another, my stomach, ears, nose, and restless eyes in others, and my working senses elsewhere. These many co-wives tear the householder apart."

The tongue pulls towards tastes, the genitals towards bad purposes. The belly pulls towards eating, a terrible calamity.

The skin pulls towards soft beds, the ears towards talks. The nose pulls towards sweet scents, the eyes go towards sights.

The working senses pull towards work; just as many wives attract the master of the house, my mind is pulled like that.

This is my condition, O Son of Nanda. How shall I remember Your pastimes?

"O Lord, let me have that great fortune, whether in this birth or another, or even as an animal, that I may become one of Your devotees and serve Your tender feet."

Whether in this birth as Brahmā or in any other existence, even if I am born as a beast or bird in Your creation...

My only hope is this: that staying in the company of Your devotees, I may serve Your feet in various ways.

"O Controller, for those who worship Your lotus feet, what is difficult to obtain among the four goals of life? Still, O Great One, I do not desire them. I am eager only to serve Your lotus feet."

O Kṛṣṇa! For one who has devotion to Your lotus feet, what is unattainable among the four goals of life?

Still, I only want service to Your feet. I have no need for any other goal.

"O Lord, I do not desire that liberation where the nectar of Your lotus feet is not present. Let the words flowing from the mouths of great souls about Your glories enter my heart. Give me ten thousand ears for this; this is my boon."

O Lord, I never want that benediction in which there is no happiness of serving Your feet.

To hear the songs of Your qualities coming from the hearts of devotees, please arrange for me to have ten thousand ears.

"I do not desire heaven, the position of Brahmā, sovereignty over the earth, rulership of the lower planets, yogic perfections, or liberation, if it means being separated from You, O Source of all opportunities."

Heaven, the place of Brahmā, the position of emperor, lordship over the lower worlds, the opulence of yoga...

...liberation and so on—abandoning Your service, I do not beg for any of these. This is my sincere vow.

"O Hari, I will become the servant of the servant of Your lotus feet. May my mind remember the qualities of the Lord of my life, may my words chant Your glories, and may my body work for You."

I was Your eternal servant, but being bound by the noose of illusion around my neck, I suffered various miseries in this material world.

Now I hope again that becoming the servant of Your servant, and worshipping You, I may obtain a trace of Your devotion.

O Lord of my life! Let my mind remember Your qualities again and again. Let my tongue sing Your name. Let my two hands find happiness doing Your work. I have offered my soul at Your feet.
